User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the Retain.ai application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

googleId.description: User's Google ID, linked from Firebase Authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

orderDate.description: Date and time when the order was placed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

transactionDate.description: Date and time of the transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SupportTicket entity.

creationDate.description: Date and time when the ticket was created.

adminId.description: Reference to User (Admin) who is handling the ticket. (Relationship: User 1:N SupportTicket)

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification sent to a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

notificationDate.description: Date and time when the notification was sent.

read.description: Indicates whether the notification has been read by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LoyaltyProgram entity.

pointsPerDollar.description: Number of loyalty points earned per dollar spent.

redemptionRate.description: Value of each loyalty point when redeemed (e.g., 0.01 for 1 cent per point).

expiryPeriod.description: Duration after which loyalty points expire (e.g., '1 year').

ProductRecommendation.title: ProductRecommendation
ProductRecommendation.description: Represents a product recommendation for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductRecommendation entity.

productId.description: Identifier of the recommended product.  Note:  Assuming Product data is managed elsewhere or via an external service.  If the product catalog were local, we'd need a Product entity.

recommendationDate.description: Date when the recommendation was generated.

FAQ.title: FAQ
FAQ.description: Represents a frequently asked question and its automated response.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FAQ entry.

question.description: The question asked by the user.

answer.description: The automated response to the question.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Uses path-based ownership for private data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores orders associated with a specific user. Uses path-based ownership. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the order.

definition.description: Stores transactions associated with a specific user. Uses path-based ownership. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores customer support tickets. Admins can access all tickets. Includes 'userId' for relating to the customer and 'adminId' for the assigned admin.

params.name: ticketId
params.description: The unique ID of the support ticket.

definition.description: Stores notifications for a specific user. Uses path-based ownership. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique ID of the notification.

definition.description: Stores loyalty program configurations.  Accessed by admins.

params.name: loyaltyProgramId
params.description: The unique ID of the loyalty program configuration.

definition.description: Stores product recommendations for a specific user. Uses path-based ownership. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: recommendationId
params.description: The unique ID of the product recommendation.

definition.description: Stores frequently asked questions and their answers. Publicly accessible.

params.name: faqId
params.description: The unique ID of the FAQ entry.

definition.description: Collection to store admin users. Existence of a document indicates admin role.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the admin user.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support a retail application with AI-powered customer support, personalized recommendations, and loyalty management. It prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability following the principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling.  Key design decisions include:

1.  **Authorization Independence:** The structure avoids chained `get()` calls in security rules. User-owned data is placed in hierarchical paths (`/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`) to implicitly enforce ownership without needing to read parent documents.  Collaborative data (if any in future features) would utilize membership maps denormalized into subcollections.  Admin roles are managed via document existence in a dedicated collection.

2.  **Structural Segregation:**  Data with different security requirements is segregated into separate collections (e.g., `/users/{userId}/notifications` for private user notifications versus `/faqs` for public FAQs).  This ensures each collection has a clear and consistent security posture.

3.  **Access Modeling:**  Private user data follows path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`).  Admin access is controlled through the `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection, leveraging existence checks.  The frequently asked questions will be public.

4.  **AI and Support Tickets:**  Support tickets are created in `/support_tickets/{ticketId}`. The tickets contain the `userId` of the customer who created the ticket. Admins can access and manage all support tickets. To enhance the AI capabilities, the frequently asked questions will be stored in `/faqs/{faqId}`. The AI will respond to the customers using the most appropriate answers based on the questions the customer asked. If the customer's question goes beyond the most frequently asked question a ticket will be created.

5.  **QAPs (Query Authorization Patterns):** The structure supports secure `list` operations.  Path-based ownership enables listing operations restricted to a specific user's data (e.g., listing all orders for a user). Segregation of public FAQs enables unrestricted listing.

6. **Denormalization:** The `userId` is duplicated in collections like `orders`, `transactions`, `supportTickets`, `notifications`, and `productRecommendations` to avoid `get()` calls when querying for entities related to a specific user.
